entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company that uses candidate self-reflection stories for hiring assessment

entities.name: Careerspan story
entities.context: A deep self-reflection exercise candidates complete as part of Careerspan's process

entities.name: Behavioral investment
entities.context: The effort and time a candidate expends, which is harder to fake than cognitive output

entities.name: Proof of work
entities.context: Demonstrable expenditure of time and attention as a scarce, valuable signal in AI-flooded markets

entities.name: Costly signaling
entities.context: Evolutionary biology principle: reliable signals must be costly enough that low-quality actors cannot mimic them

entities.name: Friction-completion
entities.context: Metric measuring whether candidates complete difficult or effortful tasks

entities.name: Engagement metrics
entities.context: Measurements of candidate effort and process alignment, prioritized over semantic content analysis

entities.name: Coachability
entities.context: Trait signaled by willingness to engage in structured self-reflection processes

entities.name: Intent signaling
entities.context: Using behavioral investment as proxy for genuine candidate interest and cultural alignment

relationships.context: Careerspan uses the story exercise to assess candidates
Both concepts address effort as signal; behavioral investment is the broader category
Proof of work is an application of costly signaling theory to hiring markets
Friction-completion is a specific type of engagement metric Careerspan should prioritize
V's position that effort signals are more valuable than content quality in candidate assessment
Willingness to engage in reflection process signals coachability
